# Therminal API

> Kalshi temperature prediction markets + NWS weather data. REST API at https://api.mostlyright.xyz

## Endpoints

All endpoints return JSON by default. Add ?format=csv or ?format=parquet for bulk downloads. Add ?columns=col1,col2 to select specific fields.

### Health
GET /health — API health status (DuckDB, Supabase, sync status)

### Series
GET /api/v1/series?active=true&limit=100 — List event series
GET /api/v1/series/{ticker} — Get series detail

### Markets
GET /api/v1/markets?series={ticker}&station={code}&from={date}&to={date}&limit=1000 — List markets
GET /api/v1/markets/{ticker} — Get market detail

### Candles
GET /api/v1/candles?market={ticker}&event={ticker}&station={code}&series={ticker}&from={date}&to={date}&interval={1|5|15|30|60|1440}&fill={true|false}&aggregate={minutes}&tz={timezone}&format={json|csv|parquet}&columns={col1,col2}&limit=10000 — OHLCV candlestick data

### Observations
GET /api/v1/observations?station={code}&from={date}&to={date}&resolution={hourly|1min}&type={METAR|SPECI}&units={raw|metric|imperial}&tz={timezone}&format={json|csv|parquet}&columns={col1,col2}&limit=1000 — Weather observations. resolution=1min returns 1-minute ASOS OMO data (NCEI DSI-6406, 2000–2026, 19/20 stations — no NYC). 1-min fields: station_code, observed_at, temp_c (integer °C), dewpoint_c (integer °C), pressure1_inhg, pressure2_inhg, pressure3_inhg, precip_type, precip_in. units= rejected with 400 for 1min; type= ignored for 1min.

### Climate
GET /api/v1/climate?station={code}&from={YYYY-MM-DD}&to={YYYY-MM-DD}&units={raw|metric}&format={json|csv|parquet}&columns={col1,col2}&limit=1000 — Daily climate reports. Report types by priority: final (3), ncei_final (2.5, source: ncei, GHCN-Daily CF6 first-published), correction (2), preliminary (1), estimated (0, source: acis).

### LOB (Limit Order Book)
GET /api/v1/lob?market={ticker}&series={ticker}&source={kalshi|polymarket}&date={YYYY-MM-DD}&from={YYYY-MM-DD}&to={YYYY-MM-DD}&interval={1-3600}&levels={1-50}&hour={0-23}&format={json|csv|parquet|ndjson} — Reconstructed order book snapshots. Returns bid/ask levels at configurable intervals. Default: interval=60 (1 snap/min), levels=10, source=kalshi. Max 31-day range. 4 concurrent requests max.

### Analysis
GET /api/v1/analysis/climate-gaps?station={code}&from={YYYY-MM-DD}&to={YYYY-MM-DD} — Find gaps in climate report history

## Parameters
- Dates: RFC3339 (2026-03-20T00:00:00Z) or YYYY-MM-DD for candles/observations. YYYY-MM-DD only for climate.
- Timezone: UTC (default), station (uses station's IANA zone), or any IANA name (e.g., America/New_York)
- Units: raw (default — °F, knots, inHg, miles), metric (°C, m/s, hPa, km), imperial (°F, mph, inHg, miles)
- Format: json (default), csv (with Content-Disposition header), parquet (Zstd compressed), ndjson (newline-delimited JSON, streaming)
- Columns: comma-separated list of field names to include in csv/parquet output

## Station codes
3-4 letter NWS codes: NYC, LAX, ORD, MDW, DEN, MIA, ATL, DFW, HOU, BOS, AUS, LAS, PHX, MSY, SEA, SFO, DCA, PHL, TPA

## Response limits
JSON: 50,000 rows max (paginated via limit/offset, default 1000, max 10000 per page). CSV/Parquet: 500,000 rows max (full result set, no pagination). For bulk ML data, use format=parquet.

## Rate limits
100 requests/second per IP, burst 20. 429 response with Retry-After header when exceeded.

## Python SDK (v1.0.3)
pip install therminal-py
Requires Python 3.11+.

Domain-split clients:
- from therminal.weather import WeatherHistory, WeatherLive
- from therminal.markets import MarketsClient
- from therminal.config import TherminalConfig

WeatherLive — real-time METAR from AWC (aviationweather.gov), schema-identical to historical WeatherHistory.observations():
  live = WeatherLive()
  obs = live.current("NYC")           # single station
  obs = live.current(["NYC", "ATL"])  # batch (one AWC request)
  obs = live.latest("NYC", hours=3)   # recent history

Config file (~/.therminal.toml):
  [defaults]
  units = "metric"
  station = "NYC"

Backward compatible: from therminal import TherminalClient still works.
ML features: from therminal.ml import WeatherFeatures, LOBFeatures, TherminalDataset

Documentation: https://pypi.org/project/therminal-py/

## Full documentation
https://docs.mostlyright.xyz
